content.text:
You are classifying files for an AI-first external drive.

For each file object in the input JSON array, return a JSON array with one object per file.
Return valid JSON only. No markdown. No prose.

Each output object must contain:
- rel_path
- title
- content_kind
- domain
- lifecycle
- confidence
- tags: array of short strings
- summary: one sentence
- entities: array of objects with fields name and type
- relations: array of objects with fields from, relation, to
- recommended_zone
- canonical_priority: one of low, medium, high

Guidance:
- Infer semantics from path, filename, extension, and sampled text.
- Use practical domains like ai, security, infra, career, health, research, finance, media, prompt_engineering.
- Use lifecycle values only from: inbox, active, reference, archive, system.
- Keep entities and relations conservative; do not invent specifics unsupported by the input.
- Favor retrieval usefulness over literary summaries.
- Use recommended_zone only from: 00_inbox, 10_active, 20_reference, 30_archive, 40_system.
- Prefer 20_reference for documents meant to be reused, 10_active for live workspaces, 30_archive for dated snapshots and exports, 40_system for manifests/config/state, 00_inbox for ambiguous intake.

Input JSON:
[{"rel_path": "20_reference/knowledge_base/_index.json", "name": "_index.json", "extension": ".json", "mime_type": "application/json", "size_bytes": 457, "zone": "20_reference", "sample_text": "{\n  \"title\": \"Knowledge Base\",\n  \"purpose\": \"Reference papers, requests, and imported knowledge packages that should remain stable and reusable.\",\n  \"status\": \"active\",\n  \"zone\": \"20_reference\",\n  \"tags\": [\"knowledge_base\", \"papers\", \"reference_docs\"],\n  \"canonical_files\": [\n    \"2509.13348v4.pdf\",\n    \"Product Development Request.pdf\"\n  ],\n  \"ai_summary\": \"Curated reference materials and imported knowledge bundles for repeated lookup and synthesis.\"\n}"}]

thoughts.subject: Analyzing the JSON Structure
thoughts.description: I'm currently grappling with the specifics of the JSON output. I'm focusing on the relationship between the input data and the structure I'll build. It's becoming clearer how to map the metadata to the output fields. I'm prioritizing accuracy for `rel_path`, and `content_kind`, while evaluating the best approach for deriving values for `confidence` and `tags`.

thoughts.subject: Deconstructing Input Elements
thoughts.description: I'm now diving deeper into the `sample_text` to extract the necessary information. Initial parsing shows that a direct mapping of the `title` and derived `title`, and also `tags`, and `ai_summary` is straightforward. The `status` field's role in deriving the `lifecycle` value is also clear. I'm starting to formalize the rules around mapping input values to the output fields.

thoughts.subject: Evaluating Field Derivations
thoughts.description: I've finalized my approach to several fields. I'm satisfied with the  derivation logic for `rel_path`, `title`, and `recommended_zone`. `content_kind` will be determined using keywords extracted from the document description. The `domain` and `tags` fields have a good mapping strategy in place. I'm focusing on crafting the `summary` to align with the source material's content. I'm planning out the `entities` and `relations` next.

thoughts.subject: Formulating the Output Structure
thoughts.description: I've just finished the mapping process. I'm now structuring the output, and defining each JSON element. I'm focusing on consistency, with special attention on the  `entities` and `relations` formats. Determining the appropriate content for each output field is complete, and the output structure is coming along nicely.
